slides.label: Funnel
slides.title: Signup Conversion Funnel
slides.prose:
The signup funnel tracks users from first visit through to paid conversion. Each stage represents a meaningful commitment — from anonymous browsing to entering payment details.

The activation step (completing onboarding) is the biggest single drop-off at 44% loss. Users who activate convert to paid at a strong 36% rate, suggesting the product delivers value once users get past initial setup.

Overall visitor-to-paid conversion sits at 6.5%, competitive for a self-serve SaaS product.

slides.label: Activity
slides.title: Signup Heatmap
slides.prose:
Signups cluster heavily around weekday lunchtimes and early evenings. Tuesday 12––1pm is the single hottest slot, likely driven by users discovering the product during work breaks.

Weekend activity is uniformly low, suggesting a B2B-leaning user base. Late-night signups (10pm+) are near zero across all days.

This pattern informs ad scheduling — concentrating spend on weekday 11am–2pm captures peak intent with minimal waste.

slides.label: Channels
slides.title: Channel Quality Radar
slides.prose:
Not all acquisition channels are equal. The radar chart compares six dimensions across Q1 (orange) vs the previous quarter (blue).

Organic search improved dramatically in volume and retention, driven by new SEO landing pages. Paid social scores high on volume but low on retention — users from Instagram ads churn 3x faster than organic users.

The ideal channel scores high on all axes. Organic search and referrals are closest to that ideal, while paid channels trade retention for volume.
slides.viz: Q1 (current)Q4 (previous)

slides.label: Revenue
slides.title: Revenue Waterfall
slides.prose:
Revenue grew from $180k base to $200k net — a healthy 11% increase. The waterfall breaks down each contributing factor.

New customer acquisition added $40k, and upsells from existing accounts contributed another $15k. These gains were partially offset by $25k in churn and $10k in downgrades.

The churn figure is the primary concern — it erodes 63% of new revenue. Reducing churn by even 20% would shift net growth from 11% to 14%.

slides.label: Health
slides.title: System Health Gauges
slides.prose:
Platform health metrics show the infrastructure is holding up well under growth. Uptime at 99.7% includes two brief outages totaling 2.2 hours — both from database connection pool exhaustion during traffic spikes.

Response time (P95) sits at 210ms, well within the 500ms target. Error rate is low at 0.3%, though upload-related 504s account for nearly half of all errors.

Throughput headroom at 62% means the system can absorb roughly 1.6x current load before needing horizontal scaling.
